steps.title: Identify the two independent clauses
steps.content:
Before the blank, “Gitlin's use of the term referenced TV studios' practice of repackaging successful narrative formulas as new content” is a complete independent clause.

After the blank, “in that era, even shows that varied only slightly from other shows still attracted sizeable audiences” is another independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the semicolon rule
steps.content:
A semicolon joins two independent clauses, so the sentence needs a semicolon to separate the two complete thoughts.

The other three choices attempt to join two independent clauses with a comma alone, creating a comma splice.

That matches B.
